images.caption:
Figure 1:Given unified multi-modal observations
(video, audio, image),EMMAextracts
identifiable physical parameters through a
physics-informed digital twin. The Liquid
Time-Constant network learns dynamics in
latent space while physics-constrained loss
enforces consistency with governing equations.
The estimated parameters enable accurate
forward simulation without requiring frame
reconstruction or segmentation masks.

Figure 2:EMMA Architecture.Multi-modal inputs (video, audio and image)
are processed through specialized pipelines
into unified temporal representations.
An LTC-NN learns latent dynamicsh​(t)h(t)and predicts physical parametersθ\theta.
A differentiable physics simulator validates
predictions, enabling end-to-end gradient
flow. The framework successfully reconstructs
digital twin drone (example shown) from
multi-modal observations.

Figure 3:Intuition for using LTC-NN
for parameter estimation.
